title: polypodium filix-femina var. molle
intro: Polypodium filix-femina var. molle, also known as Maidenhair Fern, is a variety of the Polypodium filix-femina plant, a type of fern native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Polypodium filix-femina var. molle include its ability to reduce fever, soothe wounds, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves can be dried and used in poultices for topical applications.
